pi_items.source: Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 17 (TARGET_ID="BAH999999") and verified in task.json description line 5 ('Identifier: BAH999999'). Patient created synthetically via OpenMRS REST API with this identifier during task setup (setup_task.sh line 47).

Hardcoded in setup_task.sh lines 18-19 (GIVEN_NAME="Deleted", FAMILY_NAME="Patient") and verified in task.json description line 5 ('Deleted Patient'). Patient created via OpenMRS REST API with givenName="Deleted" and familyName="Patient" in payload (setup_task.sh lines 41, 47).

Task description line 5 specifies 'https://localhost/openmrs/admin'. Verified against environment configuration: task_utils.sh line 7 sets BAHMNI_BASE_URL="https://localhost" and line 9 sets OPENMRS_BASE_URL="${BAHMNI_BASE_URL}/openmrs", making the admin URL https://localhost/openmrs/admin.
Task description line 5 specifies username 'superman'. Verified in task_utils.sh line 13 (BAHMNI_ADMIN_USERNAME="superman").
Task description line 5 specifies password 'Admin123'. Verified in task_utils.sh line 14 (BAHMNI_ADMIN_PASSWORD="Admin123").
Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 20 (GENDER="M"). Patient created with gender="M" in API payload (setup_task.sh line 42).
Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 21 (BIRTHDATE="1980-01-01"). Patient created with birthdate="1980-01-01" in API payload (setup_task.sh line 43).

pi_items.verified_value: OpenMRS provides native 'Unvoid' functionality for restoring voided patient records
pi_items.source: Web search: OpenMRS API documentation confirms that PatientService includes unvoidPatient() method that unvoids patient records and requires 'Delete Patients' authorization privilege. Legacy UI provides administrative interface to unvoid data.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves a synthetic Bahmni OpenMRS healthcare system. The target patient 'Deleted Patient' (identifier BAH999999) is created synthetically during task setup with the following demographics: Male, birthdate 1980-01-01. The task requires navigating the OpenMRS Administration interface (https://localhost/openmrs/admin) using admin credentials (username: superman, password: Admin123) to find a voided patient record and restore it using the native 'Unvoid' functionality. All metadata claims are verified against the hardcoded task setup scripts and environment configuration files. No external dataset sources are involved; all patient data is programmatically generated during task initialization.
